methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Teramount is an acquired-stage semiconductor company founded by Hesham Taha and Avi Israel, having raised $50M+ in total capital. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 3 public peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private-sector peers in Chips & Semiconductors, including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

The Peer-Median method is not applicable: while 14 same-sector peers exist within Chips & Semiconductors, filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints, making a narrow peer median unreliable. Public-Comp Anchoring is similarly not applicable—the public Semiconductors universe shows a median P/S multiple of 22.3x across the three public comparables, but Teramount's revenue is not on file, which is typical for private companies and prevents multiple-based valuation.

Given the absence of applicable quantitative triangulation methods, no valuation range can be supported by this data. The core uncertainty is disclosure: without either comparable deal terms from the private peer set or Teramount's own revenue, neither transaction-based nor multiple-based anchoring is feasible. Any valuation estimate at this stage would require either access to private deal flow or direct company financials not currently available in this dataset.
